title: sarcandra glabra
intro: Sarcandra glabra, also known as the 'Snake Root' or 'Rattan Creeper', is a perennial climber native to the tropical forests of Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Sarcandra glabra include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and various skin conditions, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal decoction or tincture, which can be used internally or applied topically to various parts of the body.
